summary: Tested 3 fixes (Purchasing send-to-warehouse button, removal of blue 'طلب من المشتريات' button, instant navigation). FIX 2 verified OK. FIX 3 partially OK (App.js PageLoader replaced — confirmed). FIX 1 has a CRITICAL backend integration bug: the new green 'إرسال للمخزن' button calls POST /api/purchases-new/{id}/send-to-warehouse but the invoice id is from a DIFFERENT mongo collection (purchase_invoices vs purchases_new) → endpoint returns 404 'الفاتورة غير موجودة' every time. Confirmed end-to-end via pytest with real admin login.

critical.issue: Returns 404 'الفاتورة غير موجودة' when called with an id obtained from GET /api/purchase-invoices. Reason: send_purchase_to_warehouse() in /app/backend/routes/inventory_system.py:449 queries db.purchases_new.find_one({'id': purchase_id}), but the frontend list endpoint /api/purchase-invoices (server.py:11402) reads from db.purchase_invoices — two SEPARATE mongo collections. POST /api/purchase-invoices (server.py:11416) only inserts into purchase_invoices, never purchases_new. Therefore the entire FRONTEND FIX 1 (green 'إرسال للمخزن' button) is broken in production. Frontend will always show toast error.

Even if collection mismatch is fixed, the backend writes status='sent_to_warehouse' on success (inventory_system.py:510). But the Purchasing.js UI checks invoice.status === 'transferred' for both: (a) hiding the button (line 578) and (b) showing the green 'محولة للمخزن' badge (line 521). So the badge will never update and the button will keep appearing after a successful transfer. Either the backend should write 'transferred' or the frontend should accept 'sent_to_warehouse'.

integration_issues.flow: Purchasing → invoice card → send to warehouse
integration_issues.issue: Button calls /api/purchases-new/{id}/send-to-warehouse but the invoice id belongs to purchase_invoices collection. Result: 404 every time, materials are NEVER added to warehouse, and status never flips. Verified live via pytest (status=404, body={"detail":"الفاتورة غير موجودة"}).

issues: Page-internal 'جاري التحميل' placeholders (>50% viewport) still appear briefly while page-level data is fetched. App.js Suspense fallback is correctly replaced with a thin top progress bar, but individual page loaders inside HR.js / Reports.js / Dashboard.js still render large centered loaders. Consider replacing with skeletons or partial UI for true 'instant feel'.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix POST /api/purchases-new/{id}/send-to-warehouse to also work with ids stored in purchase_invoices collection — either (a) make Purchasing.js create invoices via /api/purchases-new (which already has FIFO logic + send-to-warehouse), or (b) add a NEW endpoint /api/purchase-invoices/{id}/send-to-warehouse that operates on purchase_invoices and updates raw_materials + cost layers, or (c) mirror data into both collections on creation.

HIGH: Align status field semantics. Backend writes 'sent_to_warehouse' but Purchasing.js (lines 520-521, 578) checks for 'transferred'. Either change backend to write 'transferred' OR change frontend check to ['transferred','sent_to_warehouse'].
MEDIUM: Replace page-level full loaders inside Dashboard/HR/Reports with skeleton placeholders (or render shell instantly) so navigation feels truly instant per user requirement.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/routes/inventory_system.py:449 — send_purchase_to_warehouse queries db.purchases_new but the user-facing creation path goes to db.purchase_invoices. Two parallel data models without bridging is a major code smell.
/app/backend/routes/inventory_system.py:449 — endpoint is unauthenticated (no Depends(get_current_user)) → any caller with the URL can mutate inventory. Add auth + tenant scoping.
/app/frontend/src/pages/Purchasing.js:520,578 — magic string 'transferred' duplicated; should be extracted to a constant and unified with backend value.
/app/backend/server.py:11416 — POST /api/purchase-invoices does NOT mirror to purchases_new, breaking the send-to-warehouse handoff.

success_rate.frontend: 67% (FIX 2 OK; FIX 1 button visible but integration broken; FIX 3 App.js OK but page-level loaders remain)

seed_data_creation: Created TEST_Supplier_iter175 supplier and TEST_INV_175 invoice during pytest. Cleaned up after test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent fixes the collection mismatch, re-run /app/backend/tests/test_iter175_send_to_warehouse.py — specifically test_send_to_warehouse_against_purchase_invoice_id should return 200 and the invoice status should become 'transferred'. Also re-run a UI smoke test creating an invoice in /purchasing then clicking the green 'إرسال للمخزن' button and confirming (a) toast 'تم إرسال الفاتورة للمخزن بنجاح' (b) badge changes to 'محولة للمخزن' (c) button disappears (d) raw_materials quantity for the item increases.
rca of the issue: ROOT CAUSE: There are two parallel purchase invoice systems in the codebase. (1) The 'new' system in /app/backend/routes/inventory_system.py uses collection `purchases_new` and provides POST /api/purchases-new/{id}/send-to-warehouse with full FIFO cost-layer + inventory_movements logic. (2) The 'legacy' system in /app/backend/server.py uses collection `purchase_invoices` and is what the Purchasing.js UI actually creates against (POST /api/purchase-invoices). Frontend FIX 1 wired the green button to the NEW endpoint, but the invoice id passed comes from the LEGACY collection → the new endpoint's find_one returns None → 404. REPRO: 1) login admin 2) POST /api/purchase-invoices to create an invoice 3) POST /api/purchases-new/{id}/send-to-warehouse → 404. MITIGATION: Either (a) migrate Purchasing.js to use /api/purchases-new for create+list (preferred, since that path already has FIFO + tenant scoping + auth), or (b) add a new endpoint /api/purchase-invoices/{id}/send-to-warehouse that performs the same logic on the legacy collection.
